seoTitle: How to Add Roman Numerals in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to insert Roman numerals in Google Docs using numbered list presets, page number settings, and special characters for clean, professional layouts.
intro: Roman numerals show up everywhere from book front matter to legal outlines, but Google Docs doesn't have one obvious button for them. This guide walks through the built-in ways to add Roman numerals, whether you need an outline list, numbered preface pages, or a single character dropped into a sentence. By the end you'll be able to apply whichever method fits your document.

steps.heading: Apply a Roman numeral list style
steps.body: Select your text, open the Numbered list dropdown on the toolbar, and choose a preset that uses uppercase or lowercase Roman numerals (I, II, III or i, ii, iii). This is the fastest way to build a structured outline.

steps.heading: Set Roman numerals for page numbers
steps.body: Go to Insert, then Headers & footers, and open the page numbering options. You can assign lowercase Roman numerals specifically to front-matter pages like a preface or introduction.

steps.heading: Separate front matter with a section break
steps.body: Use a Section Break so your Roman numeral pagination applies only to the intro pages and doesn't carry over into the Arabic-numbered body text that follows.

steps.heading: Insert an individual Roman numeral character
steps.body: For a one-off symbol inside a sentence, use Insert > Special characters and search for the specific Roman numeral you need, then insert it directly at your cursor.

steps.heading: Polish the layout
steps.body: Combine your Roman numerals with consistent tab stops and paragraph alignment so multi-level outlines, legal briefs, or manuscript front matter look clean and professional.

faq.question: Can I use both uppercase and lowercase Roman numerals in the same document?
faq.answer: Yes. The numbered list dropdown offers both cases as separate presets, and you can apply different levels of a multi-level list independently, so a mix is easy to set up.

faq.question: Will Roman numeral page numbers reset when I add a section break?
faq.answer: Section breaks let you control numbering independently in each section, so you can start your Roman numerals fresh in the front matter without affecting the main body's page count.

faq.question: Can I insert Roman numerals on mobile?
faq.answer: The special characters and page numbering tools are primarily designed for the desktop version, so for full control it's best to make these changes on a computer.

faq.question: Does using a Roman numeral list format affect my Arabic-numbered body pages?
faq.answer: No, as long as you isolate the front matter with a section break, the Roman numeral formatting stays contained to that section only.
